title: Reservoir History Matching
source_data_quality_note: Source MD is a widefield-fluorescence fan-out artifact; principle grounded in domain literature.

E.description: Reservoir History Matching: Reservoir history matching: infer 3D permeability/porosity fields from historical production data using ensemble methods or adjoint. The forward operator produces the measurement through a 3-node primitive DAG (S.ensemble.kalman_filter_reservoir…); recovery is posed as a nonlinear_inverse problem. Difficulty tier delta=10 with effective condition number kappa_eff~100000.0; structural_uncertainty, rel_perm_uncertainty set the accuracy floor at the Omega boundary. See the forward_model field for the closed-form equation.
E.forward_model: d_obs = G(m) + epsilon; m = {log(K), phi} per cell; G = reservoir simulator
E.world_state_x: 3D permeability K(x,y,z) and porosity phi(x,y,z) fields
E.observation_y: historical production rates {q_o, q_w, q_g}(t) and BHP at N_wells wells

W.regime: Existence of the recovered 3D_permeability_field is guaranteed within the declared Omega bounds. Uniqueness holds on the measurement-supported subspace; out-of-support modes are controlled by declared priors. Stability is conditionally stable (kappa_eff ~= 100000.0); structural_uncertainty dominates the stability cliff; the remaining mismatch parameters contribute higher-order bias terms. Observation gaussian sets the irreducible data-fidelity floor.

C.error_bound: Data-fidelity bound: NRMS < 0.10 for matched model. Noise floor: O(sigma / sqrt(kappa_eff)) with kappa_eff~100000.0. Mismatch-induced bias + C2 * |structural_uncertainty|; iterative solvers achieve q=2 convergence (O(1/k^2)) near the optimum; epsilon_fn captures quality degradation across declared Omega.
